title: PS-65543: Staging site capture changes for post-freeze
body:
## ⚠️ DO NOT MERGE - BLOCKED BY CODE FREEZE ⚠️

### Overview
This PR re-introduces the site capture timezone alignment changes originally implemented in #10907. It is being left in **Draft** status per Adam's request due to the ongoing `feature/Improv` code freeze.

### Changes Re-applied
* Re-implemented user timezone conversion and `TimeZone` property mapping in `SiteCaptureViewerSystem.GetSessionsForFloor`.
* Re-added the CLDR 001 locale mapping logic for the `maya-viewer` component host page.
* Restored the parallel integration tests validating `WindowsToIANA` conversion.

### Next Steps
Leave in Draft. This will be ready for formal review and merging once the `feature/Improv` environment freeze is lifted.
author: aamacavei

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Re-introduces Site Capture viewer timezone alignment and viewer localization wiring so the Maya Viewer receives (a) an IANA timezone string via the floor sessions API response and (b) a locale-aware `lng` value from the Angular host page.

**Changes:**
- Pass a mapped `lng` locale from `SiteCaptureViewerPage` into the `maya-viewer` wrapper component.
- Populate `SiteCaptureViewerSession.TimeZone` in `SiteCaptureViewerSystem.GetSessionsForFloor` using `TimeZoneService.WindowsToIANA`.
- Add a parallel integration test asserting the floor session `TimeZone` is IANA and not a Windows timezone name; extend `TimeZoneService` with a CLDR “001”-based reverse mapping.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Core/Web Layer/WebUI/ps-ng/src/app/SiteCapture/viewer/site-capture-viewer.page.ts | Adds locale-to-BCP47 mapping and provides `lng` signal for the embedded viewer. |
| Core/Web Layer/WebUI/ps-ng/src/app/SiteCapture/viewer/site-capture-viewer.page.html | Wires `[lng]` into the `<maya-viewer>` component. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/SiteCapture/SiteCaptureViewer/GetSessionsForFloor.cs | Adds an integration test for IANA timezone propagation; adds a helper to set user timezone for the scenario. |
| Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/SiteCapture/SiteCaptureViewerSystem.cs | Maps the requestor’s Windows timezone name to IANA and returns it on each session. |
| Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/Services/TimeZone/TimeZoneService.cs | Implements deterministic Windows→IANA conversion using CLDR “001” reverse mapping. |
</details>
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: The new test method name uses underscore separators, but the rest of this file’s tests use PascalCase without underscores. Aligning the name keeps test discovery/search consistent within the suite.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This helper builds an UPDATE statement via string interpolation, which is brittle (quotes in emails/timezone IDs will break the SQL) and unnecessarily exposes an injection surface. Use a parameterized command via IDBHelper instead.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The field doc comment says the reverse lookup chooses the "alphabetically first" IANA name, but the implementation now selects the CLDR "001" (universal default) mapping. Update the comment so it matches the behavior.
review_comments.author: Copilot
